title: Bitter Chewy Jamaican Coconut Stir-fry
description: This paleo-friendly bitter and chewy jamaican dish features a unique blend of sunflower oil and sous-vide cooking techniques to create a hangover-cure-worthy meal.
instructions: Place bitter almonds in a sous-vide bag, then seal and vacuum-seal to prevent air from entering.
Cook the sealed almonds at 275°F for 1 hour using a sous-vide machine or water bath.
Remove the nuts from the bag once done, let them cool slightly, then chop into small pieces.
In a large skillet, heat sunflower oil over medium-high heat. Add chopped bitter almonds and cook until golden brown, about 2 minutes.
Pour in coconut milk and sugar-free ketchup. Bring to a simmer, stirring constantly to avoid burning the bottom of the pan.
Reduce heat to low, add paleo-friendly coconut sugar and stir well to combine with nuts and liquid. Cook for an additional minute until the mixture thickens slightly.
Garnish with unsweetened shredded coconut before serving.
